title: Hiqbah Mina Taarikh — Session 1
author: Albani Zaria
description: Introduction to the series and why studying history matters.
transcript: In this opening session, the lecture introduces the meaning of history, why documenting events matters, and the importance of preserving authentic narrations for later generations.

title: Hiqbah Mina Taarikh — Session 2
author: Albani Zaria
description: Sources and evidence in writing authentic history.
transcript: This session explains how sources are collected, checked, and compared. It emphasizes careful evaluation, bias awareness, and the duty of preserving historical accuracy.

title: Hiqbah Mina Taarikh — Session 3
author: Albani Zaria
description: Narrative structure and chronology in historical writing.
transcript: The lecture focuses on organizing events in sequence, presenting them clearly, and maintaining balance between narrative flow and evidence-based reporting.
